chapter_context.summary: A brief letter to the 'elect lady' and her children, rejoicing in those walking in truth and urging them to obey the commandment of love while guarding against deceivers.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Walking in Truth and Love (1:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 John.

outline.text: Beware of Deceivers and Final Greetings
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Beware of Deceivers and Final Greetings (1:7-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 John.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Verse Insight: 2 John 1:1**
John addresses the 'elect lady', likely a local church, emphasizing the shared bond in truth.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

key_themes.title: Truth and Love
key_themes.description: Foundational principles of the Christian walk.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
